game.name: Stormy Seas
game.history: Stormy Seas, designed by Binary Arts and released in 1997, originally features an 8x9 board with beginner, intermediate and advanced board set-ups. The game is now available under the new title Wave Breaker in ThinkFun’s All Star Logic Games collection. Our version of Stormy Seas- a smaller, 5x7 board- is inspired by that original board game.
game.board: Stormy Seas has five free-moving horizontal rows, each of which can be shifted left and right.
game.pieces: In addition to the free-moving rows, Stormy Seas contains one red boat and one purple boat. The red boat is the primary gamepiece, as the aim of the game is to dock it at the port alcove. The purple boat usually blocks the way of the red boat.
game.tomove: 1. Move a horizontal row left or right, if there is space available. 2. Slide any boat up or down. 3. Slide a boat left or right, which will potentially move several rows.
game.towin: Get the red boat to the port alcove.
game.rules: The aim of the game is to get the primary red boat to the port located at the bottom of the board. Any space containing a wave symbol acts as a raised "block" preventing a row from shifting too far left or too far right. Additionally, boats cannot traverse any space containing a wave symbol. You will need to strategically move the rows or boats to get the purple boat out of the way.
game.strategies: You will usually need to get the purple boat out of the way to the sides of the board first. For the intermediate puzzles, you may need to alternate between moving the purple and red boats several times, as the purple boat will continuously block the red boat.

references.reference: Mortensen, Eric. “Stormy Seas Seafaring Puzzle Game Review - Puzzled.” Geeky Hobbies, 30 June 2015, www.geekyhobbies.com/stormy-seas-seafaring-puzzle-game-review-puzzled. Accessed 16 May 2026.

link.description: A review of the original 1997 Stormy Seas puzzle, containing playing instructions and strategies.
